proposals.label: Security Failure as Fair Use Liability
proposals.description:
A situation concept that the adequacy of security measures to prevent verbatim output is a critical factor in determining fair use.
Encompasses: Market harm analysis, security as a legal safeguard, liability for inadequate protection.
Excludes: General copyright infringement or technical alignment research.
proposals.rationale: High frequency (61) unmapped concept linking technical security failures to legal liability frameworks, representing a distinct intersection of safety and legal discourse.

proposals.label: Sovereign AI as Strategic Independence
proposals.description:
A Desire within accelerationist discourse that nations should develop AI capabilities with minimal reliance on external actors.
Encompasses: National security, economic competitiveness, control over systems and data.
Excludes: Global open-source democratization or international collaboration.
proposals.rationale: Repeatedly cited (4) as a strategic necessity for national security and competitiveness, distinct from general 'AI for everyone' desires.

proposals.label: Law-Following AI (LFAI)
proposals.description:
A Desire within safetyist discourse that AI agents are built to rigorously comply with a broad set of legal requirements.
Encompasses: Natural-language law reasoning, policy-driven design, compliance-first architecture.
Excludes: Hard-coded rule sets or simple output filtering.
proposals.rationale: Distinct from existing safety nodes; focuses on reasoning about natural-language law rather than hard-coded constraints.
